data.content: Only one file has `File.ReadAllText`. I'll add `System.IO.Abstractions` and inject `IFileSystem` into `DataLoader`.

arguments.command: cd /tmp/sv-02f671c6b6b64f2e9ebf147f681bf73d/MyProject && dotnet add package System.IO.Abstractions 2>&1 | tail -5

data.reasoningText:
There's only one file with `File.ReadAllText` — `DataLoader.cs`. I need to:

1. Add the `System.IO.Abstractions` NuGet package to the project
2. Update `DataLoader.cs` to inject `IFileSystem` and use it instead of `File.ReadAllText`

Let me check if `System.IO.Abstractions` is the right package for .NET 10. This is the standard package for abstracting file system operations.

arguments.command: cd /tmp/sv-02f671c6b6b64f2e9ebf147f681bf73d/MyProject && dotnet add package System.IO.Abstractions 2>&1 | tail -5
